lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: What can I do yer for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A glass of your finest ale please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. I don't seem to have enough money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you recommend where an adventurer might make his fortune?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Ooh I don't know if I should be giving away information, makes the game too easy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Game? What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: This world around us... is an online game... called Old School RuneScape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, still don't understand what you are talking about. What does 'online' mean?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: It's a sort of connection between magic boxes across the world, big boxes on people's desktops and little ones people can carry. They can talk to each other to play games.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I give up. You're obviously completely mad!

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Go and talk to the bartender at the Jolly Boar Inn, he doesn't seem to mind giving away clues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can get some good equipment?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Well, there's the sword shop across the road, or there's also all sorts of shops up around the market.
